title: Integrated interpretation of NPM1-mutated AML with FLT3-ITD
learning_objectives: Recognise the characteristic features of NPM1-mutated AML.
Interpret the significance of concurrent FLT3-ITD.
Integrate morphology, immunophenotype, cytogenetics and molecular findings.
Differentiate diagnostic classification from prognostic and therapeutic implications.
clinical_vignette: A 54-year-old woman presents with fatigue, gingival bleeding and easy bruising for three weeks. Bone marrow examination demonstrates acute myeloid leukaemia. Cytogenetic and molecular studies are requested urgently.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Numerous circulating blasts with occasional cup-like nuclear invaginations. No Auer rods identified.
morphology.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow with approximately 78% myeloblasts. Blasts have moderate cytoplasm and occasional cup-like nuclei.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: Immunophenotype is compatible with AML and is characteristic of many NPM1-mutated cases.

integrated_laboratory_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukaemia with NPM1 mutation and concurrent FLT3-ITD.
question: Which statement best reflects the integrated interpretation of these findings?
options: A. This is mixed phenotype acute leukaemia because CD34 is absent.
B. This is AML with NPM1 mutation; the concurrent FLT3-ITD has important prognostic and therapeutic implications but does not change the diagnostic entity.
C. This is acute promyelocytic leukaemia because HLA-DR expression is reduced.
D. A normal karyotype excludes a genetically defined AML.
E. FLT3-ITD alone determines the diagnostic classification.
correct_answer: B. This is AML with NPM1 mutation; the concurrent FLT3-ITD has important prognostic and therapeutic implications but does not change the diagnostic entity.
explanation: NPM1 mutation defines a genetically distinct AML entity. Concurrent FLT3-ITD influences prognosis and targeted treatment decisions but does not alter the diagnostic classification.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: Absence of CD34 is common in NPM1-mutated AML.
why_other_options_are_wrong.C: Reduced HLA-DR alone is insufficient for APL.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: Normal karyotype does not exclude molecularly defined AML.
why_other_options_are_wrong.E: FLT3-ITD is prognostic rather than diagnostic.

frcpath_pearl: Differentiate disease classification from risk stratification. NPM1 defines the entity; FLT3-ITD modifies prognosis and therapy.
common_pitfalls: Assuming normal karyotype excludes genetically defined AML.
Overcalling APL based on reduced HLA-DR.
Using FLT3-ITD as the defining diagnostic abnormality.
take_home_message: Integrate morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics and molecular findings to classify AML accurately.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition
